foundation_basement.evidence: Basement appears finished in two photos with paneling and drop ceiling; no permit language in listing
foundation_basement.missing: Whether the finished basement has permits, whether egress windows exist, and any basement moisture history
foundation_basement.why_it_matters: Unpermitted finished basements can block financing, fail insurance inspection, and complicate resale for a 1972 home
questions: Are there permits on file for the basement finish?
Has the basement ever had any moisture or water intrusion?
Do the basement bedrooms meet egress code?

water_leaks.evidence: Recent roof replacement and water heater replacement disclosed in listing
water_leaks.missing: Plumbing supply line material and age
water_leaks.why_it_matters: Older 1972 home may still have galvanized supply lines that restrict flow and corrode internally
questions: What material are the water supply lines and when were they last replaced?

roof_exterior.evidence: Listing states roof replaced in 2023 with transferable warranty
roof_exterior.missing: Siding material age, soffit/fascia condition, gutter drainage pattern
roof_exterior.why_it_matters: Even with a new roof, exterior envelope (siding, flashing, drainage) determines long-term water intrusion risk
questions: What is the age and material of the siding?
Where does the downspout discharge — does it slope away from the foundation?

hidden_ownership_cost.evidence: Listing mentions oil heating; no permit or inspection history disclosed
hidden_ownership_cost.missing: Oil tank age, location (basement or buried), decommissioning status, and annual fuel cost
hidden_ownership_cost.why_it_matters: Oil heating is more expensive to operate and a buried tank can leak, triggering state cleanup funds and insurance issues
questions: Where is the oil tank located and when was it last inspected?
What was the average annual heating fuel cost over the last 3 years?

listing_does_not_prove: Roof age or last replacement date (listing mentions recent work but does not give exact date)
Foundation condition — no engineer letter or visible inspection report
Basement water history — no disclosure of any past seepage or sump pump use
Finished-basement permits and code compliance for the 2-bed basement layout
Plumbing material and age (copper, galvanized, or PEX) is not stated
Electrical panel amperage and last inspection date
HVAC system age, brand, and last service date
Water heater age and type (tank vs tankless)
Actual HOA fee and what it covers (this is not an HOA property but worth confirming)
Average monthly utility cost for electric, gas, and oil heating
Recent renovation permits pulled in the last 10 years
Seller disclosure statement or pre-listing inspection report availability
before_you_book_showing: Are the basement finishes permitted and do the bedrooms meet egress code?
Has the basement ever had water intrusion, and is there a sump pump with battery backup?
Where is the oil tank located, what is its age, and when was it last inspected?
What material are the plumbing supply lines and what year were they last replaced?
What is the amperage of the electrical panel and when was it last inspected?
What is the age and service history of the HVAC system and water heater?
Is the seller willing to share a pre-listing inspection report or seller disclosure?

photo_review.overallSummary: Photos show a 1972 single-family home with a finished basement and oil heating. Visible risks include potential unpermitted basement work, possible galvanized plumbing, and a buried oil tank that needs verification. Recommend verification before booking.

areas.name: basement
visibleConcerns: Older wood paneling, drop ceiling, and carpet over concrete — photos do not prove moisture history or permit status
whatToCheckNext: Verify moisture history with the seller

areas.name: kitchen
visibleConcerns: Galvanized supply lines may be original and could restrict flow — verify plumbing material

areas.name: exterior
visibleConcerns: Photos do not prove siding age or whether any addition is permitted
whatToCheckNext: Confirm oil tank location during showing
